title: Grandma and the new phone
summary: A young woman gives her old phone to her grandmother, who is not happy about it.

body.speaker: Mai
body.text: Grandma, this is for you. It's my old phone. It still works!

body.speaker: Grandma
body.text: Oh. Thank you, dear. But I have a phone.

body.speaker: Mai
body.text: Your phone is very old, Grandma. This one is better. You can see photos. You can call me.

body.speaker: Grandma
body.text: I call you on the home phone. We talk every Sunday.

body.speaker: Mai
body.text: Yes, but with this phone, you can call me any day.

body.speaker: Grandma
body.text: Mai, I don't want to call you any day. I want to call you on Sunday.

body.speaker: Grandma
body.text: Because on Sunday, I think about you all week. Then I call. Then I am happy. If I call every day, it is not the same.

body.speaker: Mai
body.text: I see. So… you don't want the phone?

body.speaker: Grandma
body.text: I love the phone, dear. But I love Sundays more.

grammarNotes.title: Present simple
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'present simple' cho thói quen và sự thật: I have, I call, We talk, I want, I love.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for habits and facts: I have, I call, We talk, I want, I love.

cloze.template:
Mai: Grandma, this is for you. It's my {{b1}} phone. It still {{b2}}!
Grandma: Oh. {{b3}} you, dear. But I have a phone.
Mai: Your phone is very {{b4}}, Grandma. This one is better. You can {{b5}} photos. You can call me.
Grandma: I call you on the home phone. We talk every {{b6}}.
Mai: Yes, but with this phone, you can call me {{b7}} day.
Grandma: Mai, I don't want to call you any day. I want to call you on Sunday.
Mai: Oh. Why?
Grandma: {{b8}} on Sunday, I think about you all week. Then I call. Then I am happy. If I call every day, it is not the {{b9}}.
Mai: I see. So… you don't want the phone?
Grandma: I love the phone, dear. But I love Sundays {{b10}}.

blanks.explanation: Mai is giving her old phone.
Mai says the grandma's phone is very old.
She is giving her reason: 'Because on Sunday, I think about you all week.'

translationVi:
Mai: Bà ơi, cái này tặng bà. Đây là điện thoại cũ của cháu. Vẫn dùng được ạ!
Bà: Ồ. Cảm ơn cháu. Nhưng bà đã có điện thoại rồi.
Mai: Điện thoại của bà cũ lắm. Cái này tốt hơn. Bà có thể xem ảnh. Bà có thể gọi cháu.
Bà: Bà gọi cháu bằng điện thoại bàn mà. Tuần nào chúng ta cũng nói chuyện vào Chủ nhật.
Mai: Vâng, nhưng với cái này bà có thể gọi cháu bất cứ ngày nào.
Bà: Mai à, bà không muốn gọi cháu bất cứ ngày nào. Bà muốn gọi cháu vào Chủ nhật.
Mai: Ơ. Sao ạ?
Bà: Vì cả tuần bà nghĩ về cháu. Rồi đến Chủ nhật bà gọi. Rồi bà thấy vui. Nếu gọi mỗi ngày, nó không còn như thế nữa.
Mai: Cháu hiểu rồi. Vậy… bà không cần điện thoại sao?
Bà: Bà rất thích cái điện thoại, cháu yêu. Nhưng bà thích Chủ nhật hơn.

options: No, it is Mai's old phone

questions.prompt: Does Grandma have a phone already?
Why doesn't Grandma want to call every day?
questions.explanation: 'If I call every day, it is not the same.'
questions.hint: What changes if she calls every day?

questions.prompt: What does Grandma think about all week?
questions.explanation: 'On Sunday, I think about you all week.'
questions.hint: Look at the long sentence near the end.

options: Yes, but she likes Sundays more
questions.explanation: 'I love the phone, dear. But I love Sundays more.'
questions.hint: What is the very last line?

questions.prompt: Who is older in this dialogue?

questions.explanation: 'Works' here means functions / can be used.
questions.hint: Mai is giving Grandma the phone, so the phone must be usable.

options: More contact is always more love
Sometimes waiting makes a thing feel more special
questions.explanation: Grandma values the waiting between calls, which makes Sundays feel special.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Grandma chooses to call less often because she values the feeling of waiting. Are there things in your life that lose meaning when they become too easy or too frequent?
